spacing_and_layout.global: Generous spacing. Use container mx-auto max-w-lg for mobile-first puzzle feel. p-4 or p-6 for most cards.
spacing_and_layout.buttons: Neo-brutalist tactile. All primary buttons must have `rounded-2xl border-2 border-slate-800 shadow-[0_4px_0_0_#1E293B]`. On `:active`, apply `translate-y-1 shadow-none`. Apply this to menu buttons, grid controls, and level nodes.
spacing_and_layout.level_map: Vertical scrolling list. Nodes represent levels. Alternate horizontal offsets (`translate-x-12`, `-translate-x-12`) to form a winding path. Connect nodes with a dashed SVG line in the background (z-[-1]).

animations_and_interactions.lamp_placed: Framer Motion: scale burst from 0.8 to 1.2 to 1.0 with spring physics on the lamp icon. CSS: transition background color of lit cells from white to pale yellow.
animations_and_interactions.level_win: Confetti overlay (canvas-confetti) + Slide up modal showing stars with spring animation.
animations_and_interactions.hover_states: All interactable map nodes scale to 1.05 on hover.
animations_and_interactions.audio: Web Audio API. Lamp placed: short bright sine wave 'pop' (600Hz to 800Hz). Level win: cheerful arpeggio (C4, E4, G4, C5). Error: low sawtooth 'buzz'.

components_strategy.game_grid: Use HTML5 Canvas for rendering the grid, lamps, and walls. The canvas should be wrapped in a responsive container with rounded-xl, thick border border-slate-800, and soft shadow.
components_strategy.lamp_counter: Sticky or fixed pill at the top of the game screen showing current/max lamps (e.g., 💡 3 / 5). If max is reached, pulse the counter red.
components_strategy.tutorial_modal: An interactive modal that appears before Level 1. Features the mascot image. Explains: 'Rules: Place lamps to light up white cells. No two lamps can shine on each other. Numbered walls dictate exactly how many lamps touch them.'
components_strategy.google_ads_placeholder: A distinct rectangular block (e.g. 300x250) styled as `border-2 border-dashed border-slate-300 bg-slate-100 flex flex-col items-center justify-center rounded-xl overflow-hidden relative`. Contains 'Sponsorisé - Espace Publicitaire' badge.

image_urls.description: Cute yellow 3D character, used as the tutorial mascot
Mock sponsor background for Google Ads placeholder

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and use 'framer-motion' for UI animations (bouncy buttons, winning modal).
Install and use 'lucide-react' for icons (lamps, back buttons, stars).
Install 'canvas-confetti' for the level win celebration.
Implement the Akari puzzle logic strictly in React state, but render it on an HTML5 `<canvas>` as requested. Ensure the canvas supports high DPI (scale by window.devicePixelRatio).
Use Web Audio API manually (no external lib needed) to generate synthesized sounds. Create a small utility file `audioUtils.js` for `playPopSound()`, `playWinSound()`, `playErrorSound()`.

The Home Screen should be a Level Map (Duolingo style) rather than a simple menu. Include a way to select modes (Classic, Color, Regional) and Difficulties via a top sticky header or tabs before showing the map.
The Lamp Counter should visibly decrement or show (used / max). e.g., '3 / 5 lamps used'. When max is exceeded, prevent placement or show a red error.
Enforce 'data-testid' on all game buttons, canvas clicks (if possible via wrapper), and map nodes for testing.
